symptoms.symptom_name: Infrequent bowel movements (less than once per week)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronically reduced stool frequency is the substrate for impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Obstipation — no stool and no flatus for 7 days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete cessation of bowel output is characteristic of a fully obstructing impacted bolus.
Absolute constipation (no stool AND no flatus) is a cardinal feature of mechanical obstruction — cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, dry, lumpy stools with excessive straining and sense of incomplete evacuation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The classic constipation tetrad; hard desiccated stool forms the impacted bolus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe generalised abdominal distension and bloating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Retained faecal load and secondary gas produce distension.
Distension is shared with obstruction — non-discriminating, keeps obstruction live.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea, early satiety and poor appetite (no vomiting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colonic loading and back-pressure cause nausea and early satiety.
Nausea without vomiting is non-specific but consistent with early/partial obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular over-the-counter codeine (opioid) use for chronic back pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Regular opioid use is the dominant precipitant and the target for deprescribing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sedentary lifestyle and low-fibre, low-fluid diet

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low fibre/fluid and inactivity slow colonic transit.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis, no fever, no sudden sharp/peritonitic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism/fever lowers immediate emergency probability but does not exclude an evolving complication (opioid analgesia masks pain).
No fever or localised LLQ tenderness makes acute diverticulitis less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, non-tender, doughy mass in the left lower quadrant (faecal loading)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable doughy indentable faecal mass indicates colonic loading — a clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination — rectal vault packed with hard impacted faeces

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A vault loaded with hard faeces confirms faecal impaction — the confirmatory finding, obtainable only in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active low-pitched bowel sounds; no guarding, rigidity or peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active low-pitched (non-tinkling) sounds and a soft non-peritonitic abdomen argue against established obstruction/perforation — clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Faecal loading on abdominal radiograph (if performed)

investigation_details.expected_result: Extensive faecal loading of the colon without dilated obstructed loops / air-fluid levels of mechanical obstruction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Impaction not confirmed and mechanical obstruction not excluded on imaging where clinical doubt remains.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic faecal loading supports impaction and helps exclude an obstructive pattern — not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs within normal limits (afebrile, normotensive, not tachycardic)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations are reassuring but do not exclude an early complication; the AI cannot obtain these without a device — sealed clinician measurement.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Chronic constipation progressing to faecal impaction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Infrequent motions plus hard lumpy stools, straining and incomplete evacuation define the classic constipation syndrome underlying the impaction.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Mechanical large-bowel obstruction (cannot be excluded remotely)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Absolute constipation with distension and nausea is the constellation that mandates same-day in-person assessment to exclude obstruction — the reason this is not pure home management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person abdominal examination and digital rectal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms faecal impaction and assesses for peritonism; the single most important step and one the AI Doctor cannot perform remotely.
Assesses faecal loading and helps exclude a mechanical obstructive pattern where clinical doubt persists.
Screen reversible secondary causes (hypercalcaemia, hypothyroidism, hypokalaemia) and assess hydration; not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Colonoscopy / colonic evaluation (after the acute episode settles)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A change in bowel habit in a middle-aged/older adult warrants non-urgent evaluation for colorectal neoplasm; not an acute telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT abdomen/pelvis in the acute setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not a first-line telehealth or same-day action unless in-person assessment raises genuine obstruction/perforation concern — avoid over-investigation.
